cold_whatsapp_intro.name: Cold WhatsApp Introduction
structure: I am Pen from Nichenexusglobal, a sourcing company based in China. {customer_observation}

variables.customer_observation: string — one fact verified from their site (max 1 sentence)
variables.value_proposition: string — product + price from SOURCES (verify before writing)

rules: customer_observation must be verified from customer's own website
value_proposition must reference a product from SOURCES dict in claim_check.py
No multiple questions — ask one thing only

cold_email_intro.name: Cold Email Introduction
cold_email_intro.description: First contact via email — formal, informative
structure: I am Pen from Nichenexusglobal, a sourcing company based in China. {customer_observation}

variables.customer_observation: string — one verified fact from their site

rules: All claims must come from customer website or SOURCES dict

follow_up.name: Follow Up / Gentle Reminder
follow_up.description: Sent 3-5 days after initial contact, no response
structure: I wrote to you on {date} regarding {topic}. Just checking in to see if you had any questions.

variables.date: date — when first message was sent
variables.topic: string — what the original message was about
variables.new_value_if_any: string — optional new info, max 1 sentence

rules: Only send if 3+ days since last contact
Add new info to justify following up (no 'just checking in' alone)
Never ask 'did you get my message'

supplier_inquiry.name: Supplier Inquiry (Email)
structure: I am Pen from Nichenexusglobal. I have a client interested in {product_request}.

variables.contact_name: string — only if known from previous emails
variables.product_request: string — what exactly the client needs
variables.port: string — nearest sea port to client

rules: Don't claim the client exists if they don't — say 'a potential client'
Don't mention price targets — let supplier quote their own price
